mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in retail sales indicates robust consumer demand, which translates into higher corporate earnings expectations and drives S&P 500 forward returns positively over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: If the 3-month annualized growth rate of RSAFS is positively correlated with negative 20-day SPX returns over a sustained 2-year period, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate signals a deteriorating labor market, leading to reduced consumer spending and corporate profits, which negatively impacts S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: If, after a trigger, the S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances over a 5-year backtest, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate is rising while inflation (CPIAUCSL) is also accelerating, it indicates a hawkish monetary policy stance to combat overheating, which typically tightens financial conditions and negatively impacts equity valuations over a one-month horizon.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases by >0.25% and CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth > 4%
failure_condition: If, during periods where both FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL are rising as specified, the S&P 500's 20-day forward returns are predominantly positive (e.g., mean return > 0.5%) over a 3-year period, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of post-recession recovery, a strong rebound in industrial production signals renewed economic activity and corporate profitability, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over a short-term horizon as investors anticipate earnings improvements.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month growth rate turns positive after a period of contraction
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 shows negative 5-day returns in more than 50% of instances following an INDPRO rebound signal during identified recovery periods, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in housing starts indicates growing consumer and business confidence, stimulating related industries and overall economic activity, which supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month moving average rises by more than 10% year-over-year
failure_condition: If, when HOUST meets the trigger condition, the S&P 500's 20-day forward returns are negative on average over any 4-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent job growth, as measured by non-farm payrolls, reflects a healthy and expanding economy, which underpins consumer spending and corporate revenues, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: If the 6-month annualized growth rate of PAYEMS shows a consistent negative correlation with 60-day SPX returns over a 3-year period, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When both GDP growth (GDPC1) and retail sales (RSAFS) are accelerating, it signifies broad-based economic strength and robust consumer demand, leading to strong corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 2-quarter annualized growth > 3% AND RSAFS 3-month annualized growth > 5%
failure_condition: If, during periods where both GDPC1 and RSAFS growth rates meet the specified thresholds, the S&P 500's 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 40% of instances over a 5-year period, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of high inflation, a rising Federal Funds Rate indicates aggressive monetary tightening, which increases the cost of capital and reduces future earnings valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over a short-term horizon.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases by >0.25% in a month
failure_condition: If, when FEDFUNDS rises during a high inflation regime, the S&P 500's 5-day forward returns are positive on average over any 3-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.
